sections.paragraph:
ἀφθαρσία, ας, ἡ (ἄφθαρτος, α- priv. + φθείρω) the state of not being subject to decay/dissolution/interruption, incorruptibility, immortality (‘higher Koine’ [Nägeli 41, 1; 31]: Epicurus 60, 3 [PLinde, Epicuri Vocab. 1906, 43]; Chrysipp.; Strabo; Plut., Aristid. 322 [6, 3], Mor. 881b al.; Herm. Wr. 12, 14; Wsd 2:23; 6:19; 4 Macc 9:22; 17:12; Philo, Aet. M. 27 ἀ. τ. κόσμου; OdeSol 11:12; JosAs 8:5; 15:4; Just., Tat.; Mel., P. 49, 351; Ath., R. 63, 14 al.) 1 Cor 15:42, 50, 53f; IPol 2:3. As a quality of the future life (w. ζωή) 2 Ti 1:10; 2 Cl 14:5; (w. δόξα, τιμή) ἀ. ζντεῖν Ro 2:7; ἀ. προσδέχεσθαι Dg 6:8 (cp. ἐνδύεσθαι τὴν ἀ. Theoph. Ant. 1, 7 [p. 74, 1] al.; κληρονομεῖν τὴν ἀ. 2, 27 [p. 166, 4]); πνεῖν ἀφθαρσίαν IEph 17:1; μεταλαμβάνειν ἀ. 2 Cl 14:5; ἀρχηγὸς τῆς ἀ. (of Christ) 20:5; ἀγὼν τῆς ἀ. 7:5; ἀπάρτισμα ἀ. the imperishable completed work (of the gosp.) IPhld 9:2; διδαχὴ ἀ. teaching that assures immort. IMg 6:2; ὁ τῆς ἀ. στέφανος the crown that is immortality MPol 17:1; 19:2; ἐν ἀ. πνεύματος ἁγίου 14:2.—ἀγαπᾶν … ἐν ἀφθαρσίᾳ have an undying love Eph 6:24 (NRSV), but some prefer as ref. either to those who love the Lord, and as such are now partakers of the future life, or to the Lord himself, who reigns in immortal glory. The presence of ἀ. in Tit 2:7 v.l. is prob. due to a misunderstanding of the rare word ἀφθορία. [τὸ] δ̣ὲ γε[ι]νό|μεν[ον ἀπὸ] ἀφ[θ]αρ|σίας [οὐκ ἀπο]γείν[εται]| ἀλλ[ὰ μ]έν[ει] ἄφ[θαρ]|τον ὡς ἀπὸ ἀ[φ]θ[αρσί|α]ς γ̣εγονός what is born of incorruption remains incorruptible inasmuch as it is born of incorruption Ox 1081, 14–19, s. 15f and 18f (=SJCh 89, 10–17 Coptic).—DELG s.v. φθείρω. M-M. TW.

priv. priv. = privativum (in grammar: privative, w. negating force)

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Chrysipp Chrysipp , phil., III b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

NRSV NRSV = New Revised Standard Version of the NT—List 6

ref. ref. = reference(s)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

SJCh SJCh = Sophia Jesu Christi—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 155
